summary: Co-hosts under Mauricio Pochettino, whose brave, proactive identity is starting to shine through after a bumpy 26-game tenure (including heavy friendly defeats to Switzerland and Belgium). Sharp passing out from the back, press-baiting build-up, and serious pace to counter — the highest-ranked side in a tight, even Group D.
style: Cycles between 4-2-3-1 and 3-4-2-1 but a consistent build-up: three at the back, a staggered midfield (Adams deepest, McKennie roaming), wing-backs high, and technical players (Pulisic) dropping into pockets to bait the press and play through it at tempo. Can also drop into a deeper 4-4-2 and break with pace.

counterReliance.note: Proactive on the ball, but lethal breaking with pace when they sit

strengths: Brave, press-baiting build-up that plays through pressure at high tempo
Positional fluidity to escape the press (Pulisic dropping, full-backs drifting in)
Pace to counter — Weah, plus Balogun/Pepi/Haji Wright running in behind
Firepower up front across multiple options
weaknesses: The brave build-up is turnover-prone if passes/movements are mistimed
Inconsistent results in friendlies (heavy losses to Switzerland and Belgium)

keyPlayers.name: Christian Pulisic
keyPlayers.note: The most technically gifted in the squad — drops to pull opponents out of shape; among the top Serie A chance-creators (2.0/90). The man to deliver

keyPlayers.name: Folarin Balogun
keyPlayers.note: 12 goals in 2026; eager runner in behind on the counter

keyPlayers.name: Ricardo Pepi
keyPlayers.note: Scored at the highest rate in the Dutch top flight (1.02/90) — another in-behind threat

tactics: Take control with brave passing from the back, draw the press and play through it, then strike with pace; against stronger sides, sit in a 4-4-2 and counter. The opener vs Paraguay's stubborn block is an immediate test of the pass-and-move approach.
notes: Most evenly-matched group at the tournament — only 24 FIFA ranking places separate top (USA, 16) and bottom (Paraguay, 40).
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group D (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
